title: andropogon nardus subsp. nilagiricus
intro: Andropogon nardus subsp. nilagiricus, also known as Nilagiri vetiver or Indian grass, is a subspecies of a perennial plant native to the Western Ghats of India, characterized by its aromatic and medicinal properties. The plant's roots are rich in sesquiterpenes, which have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, making it useful for treating various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Andropogon nardus subsp. nilagiricus have been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, with potential health benefits including reduced inflammation, improved skin health, and enhanced immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into essential oils, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used in aromatherapy, skincare, and traditional medicine.
